methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $174M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Oxa
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series D)

data.narrative:
Zeitview is a Series D frontier-tech company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $174M to date. The valuation universe consists of 5 private peers in the same sector (Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Oxa); no public comparables exist in this nascent category.

Given the early-stage and heterogeneous nature of the peer set, only one valuation method proved applicable. The Stage-Progression approach applied a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $174M total raised proxy, reflecting typical scaling between Series D and subsequent financing rounds. This yields an implied valuation of $261M. A triangulated range accounting for methodological conservatism and upside scenarios places fair value between $222M (low) and $300M (high).

The median point estimate of $261M should be interpreted with caution. The single-method result reflects the absence of sufficiently comparable same-stage or adjacent-stage peers with disclosed valuations, precluding median peer analysis. Key uncertainties include the trajectory of autonomous capabilities, regulatory pathway clarity, and competitive consolidation within the sector. Any valuation refinement would benefit from either additional same-stage peer data or forward revenue/unit economics visibility.
